records.snip: The co-culture oxidised Mn(II) at a combined rate of 3.4 to 9.0 e-15 mol Mn(II) cell-1 hr-1. ... 6.4e11 cells of Species A, 1.0e11 cells of Species B, for a combined yield of 7.4e11 cells (mol Mn(II) oxidised)-1 ... growth yield ~100-200 mg
records.notes: Autotrophic Mn(II)-oxidizing chemolithotroph grown ONLY as a 2-species co-culture (Species A is the dominant Mn oxidizer, ~7:1). Growth is extremely slow. Species A doubling time Td=6.1 day (reported). mu=ln2/Td = 0.693/(6.1*24 h)=0.693/146.4=0.00473 /h. Mn(II) uptake reported per cell (3.4-9.0e-15 mol/cell/h, combined). Biomass-normalized qMn derived: gDW/cell = biomass yield / cell yield = (0.1-0.2 g/mol)/(7.4e11 cells/mol) = 1.35e-13 to 2.70e-13 gDW/cell; qMn = rate/(gDW/cell): low 3.4e-15/2.70e-13=0.0126 mol/gDW/h=12.6 mmol/gDW/h; high 9.0e-15/1.35e-13=0.0667=66.7 mmol/gDW/h -> ~13-67 mmol Mn/gDW/h (approximate, wide). O2 uptake not directly measured; stoichiometry 0.5 mol O2 per mol Mn(II) from Mn(II)+0.5O2+H2O->MnO2+2H+. CO2/bicarbonate fixation confirmed qualitatively by 13C nanoSIMS (rTCA cycle in Species A). Buffered pH 6.5-6.8 (used 6.65 midpoint); unbuffered 5.7-6.3. Temp optimum 34-40C, grown at 37C.

records.notes: Reported max growth (division) rate on guanidine = 0.076 d-1. Converted to per-hour: 0.076/24 = 0.003167 h-1. Doubling time td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.076 = 9.12 d = 9.12*24 = 218.9 h. records.snip: the culture produced 3943 uM methane within 34 days ... On average across six replicates, the culture converted 13CH3-NH2 to 356 uM 13CH4 (17.8%) and 138.71 uM 13CO2 (6.9%) by Day 32 ... to 717.7 uM 13CH4 (35.9%) and 212.95 uM 13CO2 (10.65%
records.notes: Enrichment (mixed) culture, NOT axenic; Ca. M. hypatiae dominant (67% cells by FISH; 92.8% rel. abund. in SIT). Methylotrophic methanogenesis: MMA -> CH4 (+ some CO2 via oxidative branch of Wood-Ljungdahl). Rates are VOLUMETRIC (uM per culture volume over days), NOT per gDW, so no qS/qP in mmol/gDW/h can be derived (no biomass/OD given). Volumetric CH4 rate estimate: 3943 uM / 34 d = 116 uM CH4 d^-1 (6th transfer). Isotope-conversion percentages are relative to the 2 mM labeled fraction of the 10 mM MMA (356/2000=17.8%; 717.7/2000=35.9%; 138.71/2000=6.9%; 212.95/2000=10.65%; 394.76/2000=19.7%) - arithmetic matches paper. Optimal growth 64-70 deg C, pH 7.8; incubated at 70 deg C. No methane with BES inhibitor or without MMA (negative controls). No explicit specific growth rate or doubling time reported; exponential phase noted at Day 32. A related enrichment Ca. M. nevadensis (cited ref 35, Great Boiling Spring NV) is noted to yield up to 158 uM methane after 2 weeks at 75 deg C - that value is FROM another study, not this paper.

records.snip: We studied A. macleodii ATCC 27126 and demonstrated its ability to utilize SQ as a growth substrate. After 36 h of cultivation, the strain consumed approximately 64% of the SQ in the media, accompanied by the production of sulfate and sulfi
records.notes: Not a GEM/MFA paper; enzyme-discovery/marine-biogeochem study with pure-culture feeding experiments. Uptake rate DERIVED: initial SQ = 333 µM; 64% consumed in 36 h. Consumed = 0.64 × 333 = 213.12 µM. Rate = 213.12 µM / 36 h = 5.92 µmol/L/h. This is an average VOLUMETRIC consumption rate over 36 h; no biomass (gDW) quantification is given, so mmol/gDW/h cannot be computed. No specific growth rate reported (growth curve only). SQ is catabolized via the sulfo-SDO pathway to sulfite + 6-dehydroglucose; sulfate/sulfite accumulation measured but not quantified in text (Source Data file / Fig. 1f). records.snip: In LM pure culture, glucose was depleted at 27 h (Fig. S21A, Orange square) and lactate reached a maximum of 121.7 mM (Fig. S21A, Red circle)
records.notes: Glucose 2% (w/v) assumed = 20 g/L; MW 180.16 -> 20/180.16 = 111.0 mM. Depleted by 27 h -> uptake = 111.0/27 = 4.11 mmol/L/h. Lactate 121.7 mM at 27 h -> secretion = 121.7/27 = 4.51 mmol/L/h. Lactate/glucose yield = 121.7/111.0 = 1.10 mol/mol (consistent with heterofermentative Leuconostoc: ~1 lactate + 1 ethanol + 1 CO2 per glucose). Rates are VOLUMETRIC batch averages (mM/h); biomass reported only as OD600 in figures, no gDW available so cannot convert to mmol/gDW/h. This is a spatially-structured agar culture, so rates are apparent/lumped.

records.notes: Well-mixed liquid LM monoculture reached max lactate 193.4 mM (vs 121.7 mM on agar). No time-course value stated in text for the liquid endpoint, so NO rate derivable. Yield 193.4/111.0 = 1.74 mol lactate/mol glucose exceeds 1.0, implying additional carbon/substrate in the base CDM (amino acids/citrate) or measurement scope; flagged. Distinct condition from the agar record (well-mixed vs spatially structured).

records.snip: lactate content ... reaching 68.8 mM at 24 h and a maximum of 98.2 mM at 33 h ... the low amount of residual glucose (22.8 mM) at 30 h and consumption was significantly reduced over the next 6 h to 8.66 mM
records.notes: Glucose window 30->36 h: 22.8 - 8.66 = 14.14 mM over 6 h -> 14.14/6 = 2.36 mmol/L/h. Lactate 24->33 h: 98.2 - 68.8 = 29.4 mM over 9 h -> 29.4/9 = 3.27 mmol/L/h. Lactate here includes LM lactate diffusing into the co-culture spot (stated: '68.8 mM ... inconsistency with ... maximum lactate ... in the producer LM at 27 h was due to the lag in diffusion time'), so the lactate rate is NOT purely A01/B01 production - confounded, low confidence. records.med: Defined filtered-seawater medium with propionate as sole carbon source
records.cite: Johnson, Winifred M et al. Evidence for quorum sensing and differential metabolite production by a marine bacterium in response to DMSP. The ISME Jour

up.method: DOC/TOC drawdown (Shimadzu TOC-V analyzer) confirmed remineralization of the organic carbon substrate; no numeric specific uptake rate reported and no biomass in gDW given, so qS not derivable

records.snip: R. pomeroyi cells exhibited similar growth rates (0.1 h-1) on both substrates (DMSP or propionate), and remineralization of the organic carbon substrate was confirmed by the diminishing concentrations of DOC and TOC in the media
records.notes: Experimental growth rate mu = 0.1 h-1 reported directly for R. pomeroyi DSS-3 on propionate. Doubling time computed: td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.1 = 6.93 h. Carbon provided as 1 mM sodium propionate (= 3 mM carbon). No specific substrate-uptake or product-secretion rates in mmol/gDW/h were reported; the paper only qualitatively confirms carbon remineralization via DOC/TOC drawdown, and biomass was measured as cells/mL (10^6-10^7), not gDW, so qS cannot be derived. No pH stated. This is a metabolomics paper (targeted + untargeted MS) contrasting DMSP vs propionate growth; the only quantitative physiological rate is mu.

records.snip: R. pomeroyi cells exhibited similar growth rates (0.1 h-1) on both substrates (DMSP or propionate)... Methanethiol and dimethylsulfide (DMS), which are products of two different DMSP degradation pathways, were produced during growth on DMSP
records.notes: Experimental growth rate mu = 0.1 h-1 reported directly for R. pomeroyi DSS-3 on DMSP (same rate as on propionate). Doubling time computed: td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.1 = 6.93 h. Carbon supplied as 0.6 mM DMSP (= 3 mM carbon, matched to the propionate treatment). Secreted DMS and methanethiol were detected by GC (confirming both DMSP catabolic pathways) but explicitly not quantified, so no numeric secretion rate is available. No specific uptake rate reported; biomass was cells/mL (10^6-10^7), not gDW, so qS not derivable. No pH stated.

records.cite: Peterson, Eliza J.R. et al. Network analysis identifies Rv0324 and Rv0880 as regulators of bedaquiline tolerance in Mycobacterium tuberculosis. Nature
records.snip: the gene overexpression was induced concurrently with drug treatment for the approximate duration of one cell doubling (18 h)... MTB strains were cultured in Middlebrook 7H9 with the ADC supplement (Difco), 0.05% Tween80 at 37 C under aerob
records.notes: Approximate doubling time td = 18 h stated for wild-type MTB H37Rv in Middlebrook 7H9/ADC/0.05% Tween80, 37 C, aerobic. Growth rate computed: mu = ln2/td = 0.6931/18 = 0.0385 h-1. The 18 h is described as 'approximate duration of one cell doubling', so treat as an order-of-magnitude physiological value, not a precisely measured mu. This is a systems-biology/drug-tolerance paper (EGRIN + iMAT-PROM genome-scale model, drug time-kill assays); the genome-scale metabolic model and any fluxes are computational only, so no experimental uptake/secretion/MFA fluxes are reported. Middlebrook 7H9 and ADC are commercial formulations whose full component composition is not stated in the paper (medium_composition_stated=false). No pH given. Growth-rate reductions under bedaquiline are reported only qualitatively (Fig 2b), not as numeric mu values, so drug-treated conditions were not curated as separate rate records.

records.cite: Dufault-Thompson, Keith et al. Reconstruction and Analysis of Thermodynamically Constrained Models Reveal Metabolic Responses of a Deep-Sea Bacterium

up.method: model-predicted exchange flux only; no experimental uptake rate measured

sec.method: model-predicted (produced via acetate kinase ACKr in 15C and 20C models); not experimentally measured

records.snip: Each simulation was performed by constraining the biomass flux to a random value selected within the range of experimentally determined growth rates (0.110 and 0.112 for 4C, 0.254 and 0.255 for 15C, and 0.042 and 0.135 for 20C).
records.notes: Experimental specific growth rate measured for WP2 at 4C = range 0.110-0.112 h^-1 (average over 3 biological replicates), calculated from OD600 slope during exponential phase (OD600 converted to gDW via established Shewanella OD-dryweight relationship). Midpoint mu=(0.110+0.112)/2=0.111 h^-1. Doubling time td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.111=6.24 h. Units h^-1 inferred (turbidity monitored over hours; growth rates from exponential phase). This is a thermodynamically constrained GEM paper; uptake/secretion rates are model-predicted exchange fluxes (constrained to LMO-812 media, biomass fixed to experimental mu), NOT experimentally measured, so only mu is experimental. pH not stated for culture (model pH constrained 6-8). Full LMO-812 composition given by reference (cited ref 33), not reproduced in text.

records.snip: experimentally determined growth rates (... 0.042 and 0.135 for 20C). ... 20C revealed an earlier start of the exponential growth phase but a lower average growth rate than the suboptimal temperature of 4C ... 20C represents the highest tem
records.notes: Experimental specific growth rate for WP2 at supraoptimal 20C = wide range 0.042-0.135 h^-1 (large spread reflects high replicate variability under high-temperature stress). Midpoint mu=(0.042+0.135)/2=0.0885 h^-1. Doubling time td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.0885=7.83 h. Confidence medium because the reported range is broad (growth deficiency / stress at 20C). Lower average growth than 4C despite earlier exponential onset. Uptake/secretion rates model-predicted only, not experimental. Full LMO-812 composition by reference (ref 33).

records.strain: NRC-1 (Delta ura3 parent background; ChIP-seq harvest strains AKS134 empty-vector and KAD128 HpyA-HA)
records.cite: Sakrikar et al. An archaeal histone-like protein regulates gene expression in response to salt stress. Nucleic Acids Research (2021). DOI:10.1093/nar/
records.snip: The 50 mL cultures were grown in 125 ml flasks and their growth was monitored by OD600 until the time for harvesting (exponential phase: 36-50 h, OD ~0.2-0.35, growth rate ~0.059 h-1; stationary phase: ~70-140 h, OD ~1.4-1.7, growth rate ~0
records.notes: Prokaryote (archaeon) growth rate only; no substrate uptake, product secretion, or MFA fluxes reported (regulatory/ChIP-seq study). Reported exponential-phase mu ~0.059 h-1 in CM at 4.2 M NaCl (250 g/L NaCl = ~4.28 M), pH 6.8, 42 C, aerobic batch. Doubling time calculated: td = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.059 = 11.75 h. A decelerating stationary-phase rate ~0.032 h-1 (td=21.7 h) was also reported for the same culture but is not a true max growth rate. Separately, WT vs delta-hpyA mu_max were compared at optimal salt (4.2 M NaCl) and reduced salt (3.4 M NaCl): reduced salt slowed WT mu_max to 89% of the optimal-salt value, but absolute mu_max values were given only in Figure 1 (not in text) so are not captured here. Values are approximate (~) as printed. Other three papers in batch (PMC8670527 Arabidopsis root microbiome; PMC8692400 coral areal N2-fixation rates in ug N cm-2 d-1; PMC8692406 seafloor community 13C qSIP excess-atomic-fraction) report no pure-culture prokaryote uptake/secretion/growth-rate data in GEM-usable units and yield no records.
